sections.paragraph:
ξηραίνω [ξηρός] ‘cause a dry non-functioning condition’

—a. wither Js 1:11; freq. pass. in act. sense, of plant growth Mt 13:6; J 15:6; by metonymy, of ripened grain Rv 14:15; of liquid ceasing to flow Mk 5:29; Rv 16:12.

—b. in imagery and pass. in act. sense become dried-up, of a body or body part that becomes useless in a paralyzed condition Mk 3:1; of an exhausted victim of possession 9:18.
